stories.headline: Spain closes airspace to US aircraft involved in Iran war: It follows a decision by the Spanish government to deny the US use of the two jointly run military bases in Andalusia.
stories.summary: Spain has closed its airspace to US aircraft involved in the Iran war and denied the US use of two jointly run military bases in Andalusia.

stories.headline: Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law: The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.
stories.summary: A new Israeli law, strongly supported by the far-right, now allows for the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks.

stories.headline: Most Syrian refugees in Germany expected to return home in three years, Merz says: Merz’s government has taken a tougher line on refugees and migrants, amid surging support for the anti-immigration AfD party.
stories.summary: Germany's government, led by Merz, anticipates most Syrian refugees will return home within three years, as it adopts a tougher stance on immigration amidst rising support for the anti-immigration AfD party.

stories.headline:
News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar:

Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’
stories.summary: False reports, stemming from an unofficial X account, claimed Somaliland called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar, following accusations and threats from JD Vance.

stories.headline:
At least 70 people killed and 30 injured in Haiti gang attack:

Nearly 6,000 people forced to flee, human rights group says, as it criticises ‘abandonment’ from authorities

At least 70 people have been killed and 30 injured during an attack in Haiti’s bre [source: https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/30/deaths-in-gang-attack-haitian-farming-area-far-exceed-initial-estimate]
stories.summary: A gang attack in Haiti has resulted in at least 70 deaths and 30 injuries, forcing nearly 6,000 people to flee, with human rights groups criticizing authorities for their 'abandonment'.

stories.headline:
Goodbye Graaff-Reinet: South African town’s name change stirs racial tensions:

Minister’s decision to ditch town’s colonial-era identity and honour anti-apartheid activist divides residents

A South African town is divided over changing its name from the colonial-era Gra [source: https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/29/graaff-reinet-robert-sobukwe-south-african-town-name-change-stirs-racial-tensions]
stories.summary: A South African town is experiencing racial tensions over a minister's decision to change its colonial-era name to honor an anti-apartheid activist.

stories.headline: Elon Musk in a post on X says some of his Trump posts ‘went too far’ and that he regrets them:
stories.summary: Elon Musk stated on X that he regrets some of his posts regarding Trump, acknowledging they 'went too far'.

stories.headline: Swiss National Bank cuts interest rates by a half point to 0.5%:
stories.summary: The Swiss National Bank has reduced its interest rates by half a point, bringing them down to 0.5%.

stories.headline: Fed’s Powell says rate cut ‘on the table’ as soon as September:
stories.summary: Fed Chair Powell indicated that a rate cut could be considered as early as September, stating it is 'on the table'.

stories.headline: Artists left devastated after floods destroy renowned NT arts centre
stories.summary: The Merrepen Arts Centre in Nauiyu, Northern Territory, has been destroyed by recent flooding, causing significant loss for local artists.

stories.headline: Melbourne couple allegedly beat and starved woman enslaved in their home
stories.summary: A Melbourne couple is accused of threatening and coercing an Indonesian woman into domestic servitude, allegedly subjecting her to abuse and starvation.

stories.headline: WA tourism hotspots closed to Easter travellers after cyclone
stories.summary: Popular Western Australian tourist destinations like Exmouth and Coral Bay are closed to Easter visitors as they recover from Cyclone Narelle.

stories.headline: Middle East crisis live: Iran attacks tanker in Dubai; explosions in Tehran and Jerusalem amid wave of strikes
stories.summary: A tanker was attacked by Iran in Dubai, causing a fire, while explosions were reported in Tehran and Jerusalem amid a wave of strikes in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Zomi Frankcom’s brother demands audio of deadly Israeli strike but ambassador says ‘it’s in the IDF’s hands’
stories.summary: On the second anniversary of aid worker Zomi Frankcom's death in Gaza, her family is renewing calls for an open investigation and demanding audio of the Israeli strike.

stories.headline: ‘We didn’t want to be preachy’: David Attenborough’s unexpected new show – which might enrage cat lovers
stories.summary: David Attenborough's new series explores British gardens and wildlife, offering insights that may not please all pet owners, particularly cat lovers.

stories.headline: Hastings snaps up bargain rare earths hydromet plant
stories.summary: Hastings Technology Metals has acquired a new, unutilized rare earths hydromet plant in Thailand at a bargain price.

stories.headline: South Australia claim back-to-back Sheffield Shield titles
stories.summary: The Redbacks have defeated Victoria to secure their second consecutive Sheffield Shield championship for the 2025-26 season.

stories.headline: ClearVue awarded international certification on rooftop solar panels
stories.summary: ClearVue has received international certification for its metal-backed rooftop and carpark solar panels and is now seeking Australian Clean Energy Council approval.

stories.headline: After 16 years and $8 billion, the military's new GPS software still doesn't work
stories.summary: The U.S. military's next-generation GPS software, after 16 years and $8 billion in investment, is still not functional.

stories.headline: Water utility announces it's ditching fluoride—then reveals it did so years ago: The water utility highlighted unsubstantiated health concerns.
stories.summary: A water utility announced it would stop adding fluoride to its water, only to then disclose it had already ceased the practice years prior due to unsubstantiated health concerns.

stories.headline: Judge halts Nexstar/Tegna merger after FCC let firms exceed TV ownership limit: "Defendants must immediately cease" actions to integrate and consolidate the firms.
stories.summary: A judge has halted the merger between Nexstar and Tegna, ordering the companies to stop integration efforts after the FCC allowed them to exceed TV ownership limits.

stories.headline:
Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones: After operating in secrecy for years, a startup company called R3 Bio, in Richmond, California, suddenly shared details about its work last week

—saying it had raised money to create nonsentient monkey
stories.summary: A previously secretive startup, R3 Bio, has revealed its work on creating nonsentient monkey clones, having raised funds for this research.

stories.headline: A woman’s uterus has been kept alive outside the body for the first time: “Think of this as a human body,” says Javier González. In front of me is essentially a metal box on wheels. Standing at around a meter in height, it reminds me of a stainless-steel counter in a restau
stories.summary: For the first time, a human uterus has been successfully kept alive outside the body, marking a significant scientific achievement.

stories.headline: There are more AI health tools than ever—but how well do they work?: Earlier this month, Microsoft launched Copilot Health, a new space within its Copilot app where users will be able to connect their medical records and ask specific questions about their health. A cou
stories.summary: The proliferation of AI health tools, such as Microsoft's new Copilot Health, raises questions about their effectiveness and reliability despite increased adoption.

stories.headline: Meet the Man Making Music With His Brain Implant: Galen Buckwalter says brain-computer interfaces will have to be enjoyable to use if the technology is going to be successful.
stories.summary: Galen Buckwalter is creating music using a brain implant, emphasizing that brain-computer interfaces need to be enjoyable for widespread adoption.

stories.headline: 5 Best Video Doorbell Cameras (2026): Smart, Battery, AI, Budget, and Subscription-Free: Never miss a delivery. These WIRED-tested picks will help you keep tabs on your front door from anywhere.
stories.summary: WIRED has reviewed the top 5 video doorbell cameras for 2026, highlighting smart, battery-powered, AI-enabled, budget-friendly, and subscription-free options to help users monitor their front doors.

stories.headline: The Best Earplugs for Protecting Your Hearing (2026): Whether you want to sleep through the party or rock out (safely) to your favorite band, these earplugs will help block out the noise.
stories.summary: A guide to the best earplugs for 2026 highlights options suitable for both noise reduction in loud environments and safe enjoyment of music.

stories.headline: Allbirds is selling for $39 million. It raised nearly 10 times that amount in its IPO.
stories.summary: Footwear brand Allbirds is being sold for $39 million, a significant drop from the nearly $400 million it raised in its initial public offering.

stories.headline: As more Americans adopt AI tools, fewer say they can trust the results: AI adoption is rising in the U.S., but trust remains low, with most Americans concerned about transparency, regulation, and the technology’s broader societal impact, according to a new Quinnipiac poll
stories.summary: While AI tool adoption is increasing in the U.S., a new Quinnipiac poll indicates that public trust in AI results remains low due to concerns about transparency, regulation, and societal impact.

stories.headline: 15% of Americans say they’d be willing to work for an AI boss, according to new poll: According to a Quinnipiac University poll, 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to have a job where their direct supervisor was an AI program that assigned tasks and set schedules.
stories.summary: A new Quinnipiac University poll reveals that 15% of Americans are open to working under an AI supervisor that assigns tasks and manages schedules.

stories.headline: Food shock is inevitable due to the Iran war \u2013 and it could get bad
stories.summary: Even if the conflict in the Middle East ends today, higher fuel, fertiliser and pesticide prices will lead to a food shock in the coming months.

stories.headline: AI data centres can warm surrounding areas by up to 9.1\u00b0C
stories.summary: Hundreds of millions of people live close enough to data centres used to power AI to feel warmer average temperatures in their local area.

stories.headline: The Shroud of Turin bears DNA from many people, plants and animals
stories.summary: Researchers have identified genetic material from a vast range of organisms contaminating the shroud, said to have wrapped Jesus's body, further complicating the question of the cloth's true origin.

stories.headline: Millions of new solar system objects to be found and 'filmed in technicolor' -- studies predict
stories.summary: Astronomers predict millions of new solar system objects will be detected by a new facility coming online later this year.

stories.headline: Guardrails, education urged to protect adolescent AI users
stories.summary: A new report emphasizes the nuanced effects of artificial intelligence on adolescents and calls for developers to prioritize features that protect young people.

stories.headline: Molecular link between air pollution and pregnancy risks
stories.summary: A new study found that exposure to specific tiny particles in air pollution during pregnancy is associated with an increased risk of various negative birth outcomes.

stories.headline: STAT+: Pharma giant Takeda to lay off nearly 250 workers in latest job cutting
stories.summary: Takeda Pharmaceuticals, the largest biopharma employer in Massachusetts, will lay off 250 workers in Cambridge starting in July.

stories.headline: STAT+: Doctors Without Borders calls Gilead \u2018unconscionable\u2019 for refusing to sell HIV prevention drug to organization
stories.summary: Doctors Without Borders has criticized Gilead Sciences for refusing to provide a "limited" supply of lenacapavir, an HIV prevention drug, after months of negotiations.

stories.headline: STAT+: Early signs of Alzheimer\u2019s often go undetected. These researchers want to change that
stories.summary: Scientists are using AI to analyze changes in brain scans to predict Alzheimer's with nearly 93 percent accuracy, aiming to detect the disease earlier.

stories.headline: Kuwaiti oil tanker struck off Dubai
stories.summary: An Iranian drone reportedly struck a Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai, causing crude prices to rise and escalating tensions in the region. This incident is one of the most significant attacks on a vessel in the past month.

stories.headline: Foreign central banks sell US Treasuries in wake of Iran war
stories.summary: International official holdings of US Treasuries at the New York Federal Reserve have fallen to their lowest level since 2012, indicating a shift by foreign central banks following the Iran conflict.

stories.headline: Swiss lawmakers signal compromise on $22bn UBS capital plan
stories.summary: Swiss lawmakers are signaling a potential compromise on UBS's $22 billion capital plan, with a decision possibly in April and the foreign capital requirements component moving to parliament for debate.

stories.headline: Goldman Still Sees Gold at $5,400 By Year-End Despite Downturn
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs maintains its bullish outlook on gold, projecting renewed gains by the end of 2026 and a price of $5,400 despite a recent selloff.

stories.headline: Stock Futures, Bonds Advance on Trump War Report: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Equity-index futures rose and oil erased gains after reports indicated President Trump is willing to end the US military campaign against Iran, even if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed.

stories.headline: Abu Dhabi’s 2PointZero Splashes Out $2.25 Billion in US Gas Deal
stories.summary: A company overseen by an Abu Dhabi royal has agreed to purchase a US gas infrastructure firm for $2.25 billion, demonstrating continued overseas dealmaking by Gulf firms.

stories.headline: After Iran, gold is looking less glittery: Is the yellow metal the new crypto?
stories.summary: Following recent events involving Iran, the traditional safe-haven status of gold is being questioned, with some comparing its volatility to cryptocurrency.

stories.headline: How Iran is making a mint from Donald Trump’s war: China is helping the Revolutionary Guards profit from Iranian crude
stories.summary: China is reportedly assisting Iran's Revolutionary Guards in profiting from Iranian crude oil, despite the ongoing conflict and sanctions.

stories.headline: How high could global inflation go?: With luck, the Iran war won’t cause a recession. But the surge in energy prices will push up the cost of living
stories.summary: While the Iran conflict may not lead to a recession, the resulting surge in energy prices is expected to significantly increase the cost of living and global inflation.

stories.headline: America Should Beware of Economic Hubris
stories.summary: Despite its strong performance, the US economy may not be immune to the negative economic ripple effects of the Iran war, including higher energy and borrowing costs.

stories.headline: Why Iran’s Escalation Strategy Is Likely to Backfire
stories.summary: Iran's current escalation strategy in the conflict with the United States and Israel, intended to demonstrate resilience, may ultimately backfire economically.

stories.headline: The Sad Demise of China’s Economic Debate
stories.summary: China's previously open forum for economic policy debate is diminishing, as evidenced by the changing nature of the China Development Forum, signaling a less open society.

stories.headline: Grade Caps are Not a Good Solution to Grade Inflation
stories.summary: Despite widespread grade inflation, particularly at institutions like Harvard where two-thirds of undergraduate grades are A's, grade caps are argued not to be an effective solution.

stories.headline: Do Parents Propagate Inequality Among Children?
stories.summary: Research using data from Chinese and Swedish twins explores how parental behavior influences within-family inequality among children.

stories.headline: Claudia Goldin and the WNBA
stories.summary: Nobel laureate Claudia Goldin, the first woman to win a solo Nobel in economics, accepted one of only three invitations she received to advise the WNBA.
